title: SkillStack: A Procedurally Generated Benchmark for Measuring Compositional Cognitive Skill Gaps in Large Language Models
description: We propose SkillStack, a procedurally generated benchmark that systematically evaluates how well LLMs compose multiple cognitive skills. The benchmark defines 8 atomic cognitive primitives (arithmetic, comparison, counting, logical deduction, spatial reasoning, string manipulation, temporal reasoning, set operations) and tests all 28 pairwise and 56 triple combinations with automatically verifiable answers. By measuring the 'composition gap' — the performance drop when skills must be combined versus used in isolation — SkillStack reveals which skill combinations LLMs struggle with and quantifies how much of their apparent reasoning is genuine composition versus pattern matching.
motivation: Real-world tasks require combining multiple cognitive skills simultaneously, yet existing LLM benchmarks predominantly test skills in isolation. Recent work (GSM-Symbolic, Skill-Mix) has shown that LLMs experience 20-65% accuracy drops when tasks become compositionally complex, even when individual skills are near-perfect. However, no existing benchmark systematically measures this compositional gap across a controlled set of cognitive primitives with verifiable answers. Understanding which skill compositions fail and why is critical for knowing when LLMs can be trusted on complex real-world tasks.
proposed_approach: SkillStack uses a template-based procedural generation framework to create test items at three composition levels: single-skill (baseline), pairwise (28 combinations), and triple (56 combinations). Each item has a deterministic ground-truth answer computed by a solver, enabling fully automatic evaluation without LLM-as-judge. The procedural generation ensures contamination resistance (fresh instances every run) and controlled difficulty (component skill difficulty is calibrated to be consistent across compositions). We evaluate 10-15 models across families and sizes, using direct and chain-of-thought prompting, to produce Skill Interaction Matrices, composition scaling laws, and a taxonomy of easy vs. hard compositions.

related_work.title: Skill-Mix: a Flexible and Expandable Family of Evaluations for AI Models
related_work.authors: Dingli Yu, Simran Kaur, Arushi Gupta, Jonah Brown-Cohen, Anirudh Goyal, Sanjeev Arora
related_work.difference: Skill-Mix tests language/stylistic skills (metaphor, irony) in text generation with LLM-as-judge evaluation. SkillStack tests cognitive/reasoning skills with automatically verifiable answers and procedural generation.

related_work.title: Can Models Learn Skill Composition from Examples?
related_work.authors: Haoyu Zhao, Simran Kaur, Dingli Yu, Anirudh Goyal, Sanjeev Arora
related_work.difference: Studies whether fine-tuning on low-k compositions transfers to higher-k. SkillStack provides a systematic benchmark for measuring the composition gap across cognitive skills, complementing their training-focused analysis.

related_work.title: GSM-Symbolic: Understanding the Limitations of Mathematical Reasoning in Large Language Models
related_work.authors: Iman Mirzadeh, Keivan Alizadeh, Hooman Shahrokhi, Oncel Tuzel, Samy Bengio, Mehrdad Farajtabar
related_work.difference: GSM-Symbolic tests perturbation robustness within a single skill (math). SkillStack extends this to systematic multi-skill composition across 8 cognitive primitives.

related_work.title: How Is LLM Reasoning Distracted by Irrelevant Context? An Analysis Using a Controlled Benchmark
related_work.authors: Minglai Yang, Ethan Huang, Liang Zhang, Mihai Surdeanu, William Yang Wang, Liangming Pan
related_work.difference: GSM-DC measures distraction robustness within math reasoning. SkillStack measures the orthogonal dimension of skill composition across diverse cognitive primitives.

related_work.title: Beyond the Imitation Game: Quantifying and Extrapolating the Capabilities of Language Models
related_work.difference: BIG-Bench provides 204 independently-authored tasks without systematic control over skill composition. SkillStack provides structured, controlled composition testing with shared cognitive primitives.

related_work.title: Lost in Benchmarks? Rethinking Large Language Model Benchmarking with Item Response Theory
related_work.authors: Jiayi Zhou, Kaijie Huang, et al.
related_work.difference: PSN-IRT diagnoses quality of existing benchmark items. SkillStack is a new benchmark designed with controlled difficulty and procedural generation from the ground up.

related_work.title: LiveBench: A Challenging, Contamination-Limited LLM Benchmark
related_work.difference: LiveBench uses monthly-updated questions from recent sources. SkillStack uses procedural generation from templates, which is more scalable and enables controlled experimental design.

related_work.title: How and Why LLMs Generalize: A Fine-Grained Analysis of LLM Reasoning from Cognitive Behaviors to Low-Level Patterns
related_work.authors: Haoyue Bai, Yiyou Sun, Wenjie Hu, Shi Qiu, Maggie Ziyu Huan, Peiyang Song, Robert Nowak, Dawn Song
related_work.difference: An analysis paper studying how cognitive behaviors change during training. SkillStack provides a reusable benchmark measuring skill composition at evaluation time.

hypothesis: LLM performance on tasks requiring K composed cognitive skills degrades super-linearly with K. The composition gap is larger for sequentially-dependent skill pairs (e.g., arithmetic then comparison) than for parallel pairs, larger models have smaller but non-zero gaps, and chain-of-thought prompting partially closes the gap by decomposing composed tasks into sequential single-skill steps.
success_criteria: Statistically significant composition gap (p < 0.01) across ≥80% of skill pairs for ≥5 models
At least 3 qualitatively distinct composition difficulty patterns in the Skill Interaction Matrix
Composition gap varies meaningfully (>10 pp) across model families, sizes, or prompting strategies
Test-retest reliability (r > 0.90) for composition gap measurements across independently generated instance sets
